message: Refine FrankenTUI bead graph: test logging + hardening flow

files.patch:
@@ -414,17 +414,17 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","title":"Refactor search surface to structural responsive layout primitives","description":"Objective:
Refactor search/results/detail surface to use explicit structural responsive primitives instead of ad-hoc breakpoint branching.

Deliverables:
- Structured layout split definitions by breakpoint.
- Reduced conditional complexity in render path.
- Maintained behavior for pane selection and detail visibility.

Why this matters:
Structural responsiveness improves maintainability and reduces logic bugs in medium/narrow transitions.

Done when:
Search surface layout behavior is driven by explicit topology rules.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.023049319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:28:34.013592415Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:28:34.013568721Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.023049319Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:05.523337936Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","title":"Implement responsive visibility policy for controls, hints, and split affordances","description":"Objective:
Define and enforce visibility rules for optional controls/hints/handles per breakpoint and degradation context.

Deliverables:
- Visibility matrix for non-essential UI elements.
- Consistent hide/show logic with clear rationale.
- Safeguards against inaccessible actions when elements are hidden.

Why this matters:
Responsive UX quality depends on removing clutter without removing capability.

Done when:
Visibility behavior is intentional, testable, and documented in-code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.038448628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:13.582105361Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:13.582079223Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.038448628Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:05.984152806Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:06.216534068Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","title":"Add ultra-narrow terminal fallback UX policy","description":"Objective:
Define safe behavior for very constrained terminals (e.g., <= 60 cols or low height) to prevent unusable layouts.

Deliverables:
- Minimal viable layout mode and truncation rules.
- Priority ordering for content and controls in constrained space.
- No-panic behavior for extreme dimensions.

Why this matters:
Robust terminal apps degrade gracefully even under unusually small dimensions.

Done when:
Ultra-narrow scenarios remain functional and stable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.486515561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:47:33.751226626Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:47:33.751203603Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.486515561Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:06.938671845Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.174002333Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","title":"Reconcile hit-region and focus invariants under new responsive topologies","description":"Objective:
Ensure mouse hit regions, focus transitions, and split-handle interactions remain correct after structural layout changes.

Deliverables:
- Updated hit-region recording logic where needed.
- Focus management behavior checks for all topologies.
- Edge-case handling for narrow-mode pane switching.

Why this matters:
Layout refactors can silently break interaction semantics even when rendering looks correct.

Done when:
Interaction invariants hold across all responsive modes.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.600076499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:06.690095388Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.600076499Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:06.454348013Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:06.690043441Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","title":"Reconcile hit-region and focus invariants under new responsive topologies","description":"Objective:
Ensure mouse hit regions, focus transitions, and split-handle interactions remain correct after structural layout changes.

Deliverables:
- Updated hit-region recording logic where needed.
- Focus management behavior checks for all topologies.
- Edge-case handling for narrow-mode pane switching.

Why this matters:
Layout refactors can silently break interaction semantics even when rendering looks correct.

Done when:
Interaction invariants hold across all responsive modes.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.600076499Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:04:22.714160176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.600076499Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:06.454348013Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:06.690043441Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","title":"Refactor analytics surface responsive structure and tab/header behavior","description":"Objective:
Apply structured responsive layout principles to analytics surface header/content/footer composition.

Deliverables:
- Breakpoint-aware analytics header/tab layout adjustments.
- Content region structural behavior for constrained widths.
- Footer/status behavior consistency with search surface conventions.

Why this matters:
Analytics surface should remain equally usable and coherent across terminal sizes.

Done when:
Analytics layout adapts predictably with no brittle breakpoint-specific hacks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.859098174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:34:37.838389051Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:34:37.838365517Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:14.859098174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:05.756287998Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","title":"Responsive layout regression suite across size matrix and interaction invariants","description":"Objective:
Create comprehensive tests that validate responsive behavior across representative terminal sizes and interaction pathways.

Deliverables:
- Unit tests for topology selection, pane-visibility policy, and hit-region/focus invariants.
- E2e size-sweep scripts (small/medium/wide/very large, including ultra-narrow fallback) with keyboard and mouse interaction flows.
- Structured logging artifacts for each scenario (resolved topology, visible panes, focus transitions, hit-target map, assertion outcomes, timings).
- Diffable regression summaries highlighting behavior changes by breakpoint.

Why this matters:
Responsive logic is high-risk for subtle regressions; broad, deterministic unit+e2e coverage with logs is essential for trust.

Done when:
Responsive behavior is locked in by comprehensive automated coverage and actionable diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:15.068136009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:22.792533235Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:15.068136009Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.414365619Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.664953474Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:07.915432798Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:08.248432103Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:08.482471786Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.5","title":"Add input smoothness pass (event coalescing + hover stabilization UX)","description":"Workstream .5: Input smoothness pass (event coalescing + hover stabilization UX).

Status note:
This workstream is marked closed with reported delivery of drag jitter filtering + hover stabilization tests. It remains documented here as a completed pillar of the overall UX revelation program.

Completed intent:
- Reduce pointer jitter and drag noise.
- Improve predictability of hover/selection interactions.
- Add focused tests to lock in behavior.

Program dependency context:
Downstream work should assume this baseline exists and build on it rather than re-solving the same interaction stability layer.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.952817550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T22:54:58.624889833Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T22:49:00.071932518Z","close_reason":"Completed: drag jitter filter + hover stabilization, 6 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:10:35.952817550Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","title":"Program hardening and release gates for FrankenTUI UX overhaul","description":"Workstream .6: program-level hardening, integration validation, and release closure for the FrankenTUI UX overhaul.

Purpose:
Even strong feature work can regress UX quality if cross-workstream interactions are not validated holistically. This workstream ensures the combined system is stable, fast, and operationally understandable before epic closure.

In scope:
- Cross-workstream integration checks.
- Quality gate execution and bug-fix loop.
- Performance and reliability regression detection.
- Comprehensive unit-test and e2e script execution with structured logging artifacts for auditability.
- Final operator-facing documentation and closeout criteria.

Out of scope:
- Net-new feature expansion unrelated to closure quality.

Success signal:
All prerequisite workstreams are integrated, quality gates are green, test/e2e logs are complete, and closure notes provide clear handoff context for future contributors.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:24.515422001Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:08.715674159Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:08.946783643Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:09.185508847Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:09.420743046Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closeout guidance: do not close this workstream based solely on passing unit tests. Require integrated manual scenarios and performance sanity checks. Any unresolved UX ambiguity should become explicit follow-up beads before epic closure.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:36Z"}]}
+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","title":"Program hardening and release gates for FrankenTUI UX overhaul","description":"Workstream .6: program-level hardening, integration validation, and release closure for the FrankenTUI UX overhaul.

Purpose:
Even strong feature work can regress UX quality if cross-workstream interactions are not validated holistically. This workstream ensures the combined system is stable, fast, and operationally understandable before epic closure.

In scope:
- Cross-workstream integration checks.
- Quality gate execution and bug-fix loop.
- Performance and reliability regression detection.
- Comprehensive unit-test and e2e script execution with structured logging artifacts for auditability.
- Final operator-facing documentation and closeout criteria.

Out of scope:
- Net-new feature expansion unrelated to closure quality.

Success signal:
All prerequisite workstreams are integrated, quality gates are green, test/e2e logs are complete, and closure notes provide clear handoff context for future contributors.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:05:32.778845681Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:55:30.455380974Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":206,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closeout guidance: do not close this workstream based solely on passing unit tests. Require integrated manual scenarios and performance sanity checks. Any unresolved UX ambiguity should become explicit follow-up beads before epic closure.","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.1","title":"Assemble cross-workstream integration checklist and execution plan","description":"Objective:
Create one integration checklist early that validates interactions among palette, resize evidence, cockpit, and responsive layout changes, and translates them into executable test runs.

Deliverables:
- Cross-workstream scenario matrix (normal, stress, edge) with explicit expected outcomes.
- Mapping from scenarios to required unit suites and e2e scripts across .1/.2/.3/.4 workstreams.
- Canonical structured-log schema for integration/e2e runs (scenario_id, mode, event stream, timing, assertion outcomes).
- Triage playbook for first-failure diagnosis and ownership of each unresolved gap.

Why this matters:
Cross-feature interactions are where subtle regressions hide; defining execution and logging contracts up front prevents late-stage ambiguity.

Done when:
The checklist is executable, repeatable, and directly consumed by .6.2 and .6.5 validation runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:27.182138860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:52.508594925Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.1","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:27.182138860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","title":"Run quality-gate sweep and resolve all blockers for UX overhaul branch state","description":"Objective:
Run mandatory quality gates and drive all discovered issues to resolution for the integrated overhaul state.

Deliverables:
- cargo check --all-targets clean.
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean.
- cargo fmt --check clean.
- Comprehensive unit-test sweep for palette, resize evidence, cockpit, and responsive layout workstreams.
- Deterministic e2e script execution per integration checklist with structured run logs captured for each scenario.
- Failure triage notes linking each broken assertion to root-cause bead/fix.

Why this matters:
Foundational quality gates plus explicit unit/e2e verification prevent integration debt and silent behavior drift.

Done when:
All quality gates and required unit/e2e suites are green, with detailed logs available for audit and regression comparison.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.265644634Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:59.362691287Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:54.562763016Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:56.468767067Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:57.920779470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:58.924458745Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.4.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:02:59.362639200Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.265644634Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:10.644843285Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","title":"Update operator-facing docs and in-repo rationale for new UX behavior","description":"Objective:
Document the new runtime/inspector/palette/responsive behavior so future contributors understand intent without external planning artifacts.

Deliverables:
- Updated internal documentation snippets relevant to changed behavior.
- Notes on tradeoffs and intentional design decisions.
- Pointers to key test coverage and diagnostics.

Why this matters:
Self-documenting systems reduce future rework and repeated design debates.

Done when:
Future contributors can reason about the overhaul from repository artifacts alone.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.364062474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:02.610536013Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.364062474Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:11.377007918Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:02.610415739Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.3","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:11.613739719Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","title":"Run UBS scan and resolve safety/reliability findings","description":"Objective:
Run UBS on changed files and close out any substantial safety, reliability, or bug-risk findings.

Deliverables:
- UBS scan output review.
- Fixes for high-impact findings.
- Follow-up beads for deferred lower-priority findings (if any).

Why this matters:
Independent static review catches classes of issues that targeted tests may miss.

Done when:
UBS findings are either resolved or explicitly tracked with rationale.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.678488755Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:02.114614718Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:58:43.678488755Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T23:00:11.136230480Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.4","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T00:03:02.114501507Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mfw3.6.5","title":"Execute performance/regression envelope checks for interactive UX","description":"Objective:
Validate that integrated UX changes preserve or improve responsiveness under representative workloads.

Deliverables:
- Performance checks for search/detail/cockpit/palette interactions.
- Resize-stress and degraded-mode checks across standard, inline, fullscreen, and macro-related paths.
- Deterministic performance e2e scripts with structured telemetry logs (latency, frame budget pressure, degradation level, decision transitions).
- Baseline-vs-candidate regression report with explicit thresholds and documented tradeoffs.

Why this matters:
A UX overhaul is successful only if perceived and measured responsiveness improve without sacrificing reliability.

Done when:

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","title":"P2.2 Indexer multi-root orchestration","description":"# Indexer Multi-Root Orchestration

## Context
The indexer needs to build a list of scan roots and orchestrate scanning across them, injecting provenance appropriately.

## Location
src/indexer/mod.rs

## Current Flow
1. Build single ScanContext with data_dir
2. For each connector, call scan()
3. Persist results

## New Flow
1. Build list of scan roots:
   - Local roots (from watch_roots() or equivalent)
   - Remote mirror roots (from sources table)
2. Group roots by connector affinity
3. For each connector:
   - For each relevant root:
     - Create ScanContext with that root
     - Call scan()
     - Inject provenance into results
     - Persist with source_id

## Implementation

### Build Scan Roots List
\`\`\`rust
fn build_scan_roots(storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Vec<ScanRoot> {
    let mut roots = Vec::new();
    
    // Add local roots (existing logic from watch_roots())
    for local_root in local_default_roots() {
        roots.push(ScanRoot {
            path: local_root,
            origin: Origin::local(),
            platform: Some(Platform::current()),
            workspace_rewrites: vec![],
        });
    }
    
    // Add remote mirror roots
    for source in storage.list_sources()? {
        if source.kind == SourceKind::Ssh {
            let mirror_root = data_dir.join("remotes").join(&source.id).join("mirror");
            if mirror_root.exists() {
                roots.push(ScanRoot {
                    path: mirror_root,
                    origin: Origin {
                        source_id: source.id.clone(),
                        kind: source.kind,
                        host: source.host_label.clone(),
                    },
                    platform: source.platform,
                    workspace_rewrites: parse_rewrites(&source.config),
                });
            }
        }
    }
    
    roots
}
\`\`\`

### Connector Affinity
Not all roots apply to all connectors. For example:
- ClaudeCodeConnector cares about ~/.claude or remotes/*/mirror/**/.claude
- AiderConnector cares about .aider.chat.history.md anywhere

For now: let connectors handle root relevance via their detect() logic.

### Provenance Injection
After connector.scan() returns:
\`\`\`rust
for conv in conversations.iter_mut() {
    conv.metadata["cass"] = json!({
        "origin": {
            "source_id": scan_root.origin.source_id,
            "kind": scan_root.origin.kind.as_str(),
            "host": scan_root.origin.host,
        }
    });
}
\`\`\`

## Dependencies
- P2.1 (ScanContext extended)
- P1.3 (storage accepts source_id)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Indexer builds list of scan roots
- [ ] Local roots work as before
- [ ] Provenance injected into metadata
- [ ] Results persisted with correct source_id
- [ ] Tests verify multi-root behavior","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-16T05:57:31.805169Z","updated_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","closed_at":"2025-12-16T16:33:11.166283Z","close_reason":"Implemented multi-root orchestration with build_scan_roots(), inject_provenance(), and provenance extraction in persist::map_to_internal(). All 294 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1mv","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1v7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","title":"Task 4: Update template.rs and lib.rs integration","description":"# Objective
Update the HtmlExporter and lib.rs export flow to use the new MessageGroup system.

## Changes Required

### 1. HtmlExporter::export_messages signature update
In src/html_export/template.rs:

```rust
pub fn export_messages(
    &self,
    title: &str,
    groups: &[MessageGroup],  // Changed from &[Message]
    metadata: TemplateMetadata,
    password: Option<&str>,
) -> Result<String, TemplateError>
```

### 2. lib.rs run_export_html integration
Around line 10302, after creating Vec<Message>:

```rust
// OLD:
let messages: Vec<Message> = raw_messages.iter()...collect();

// NEW:
let flat_messages: Vec<Message> = raw_messages.iter()...collect();
let message_groups = group_messages_for_export(flat_messages);

// Update metadata message count
let metadata = TemplateMetadata {
    message_count: message_groups.len(), // or sum of all messages
    ...
};

// Pass groups to exporter
let html = exporter.export_messages(title, &message_groups, metadata, final_password.as_deref())?;
```

### 3. Update RenderOptions usage
Ensure render options properly flow through to grouped rendering:
- show_tool_calls flag should control whether badges are shown
- show_timestamps applies to group primary message
- collapse_threshold applies to group primary content

### 4. Backwards Compatibility
We do NOT care about backwards compatibility (per AGENTS.md). Change the API directly, update all call sites.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] export_messages accepts MessageGroup slice
- [ ] lib.rs calls grouping function before export
- [ ] Metadata reflects correct counts
- [ ] All compile errors resolved
- [ ] Integration works end-to-end","notes":"## Metadata Count Clarification

The TemplateMetadata.message_count field should reflect:
- **Total messages** (sum of all messages in all groups), NOT group count
- This maintains backwards compatibility with existing exports
- Add new field if needed: group_count

```rust
let metadata = TemplateMetadata {
    message_count: message_groups.iter()
        .map(|g| 1 + g.tool_calls.len()) // primary + tools
        .sum(),
    // Or add: group_count: message_groups.len(),
    ...
};
```

## CLI Flag Behavior

### --include-tools flag
- TRUE (default): Show tool badges in message headers
- FALSE: Hide all tool badges, show only message content

### --show-timestamps flag
- Applies to group primary message timestamp
- Tool individual timestamps not shown (too noisy)

### --no-cdns flag
- Unchanged behavior
- All CSS/JS still inlined when CDNs disabled

### --theme flag
- Unchanged: "dark" or "light" default
- Applied to grouped messages same as before

## OpenCode Session Handling

The existing `load_opencode_session_for_export()` returns raw messages.
These still need to go through the grouping function:

```rust
if is_opencode {
    match load_opencode_session_for_export(session_path) {
        Ok((title, start, end, msgs)) => {
            // msgs are raw serde_json::Value, convert to Message first
            let flat_messages = msgs.iter()
                .filter_map(|m| convert_opencode_message(m))
                .collect();
            let message_groups = group_messages_for_export(flat_messages, AgentFormat::OpenCode);
            // ...
        }
    }
}
```

## Error Handling

If grouping fails (shouldn't, but for safety):
```rust
let message_groups = group_messages_for_export(flat_messages, agent_format);
if message_groups.is_empty() && !flat_messages.is_empty() {
    warn!(
        flat_count = flat_messages.len(),
        "Grouping produced no groups from non-empty messages, falling back"
    );
    // Fallback: each message becomes its own group
    message_groups = flat_messages.into_iter()
        .map(|m| MessageGroup::new(m, MessageGroupType::from_role(&m.role)))
        .collect();
}
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:57:11.883247813Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:17:09.152702697Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:17:09.152606859Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","depends_on_id":"2jxn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:40Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1v5c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:40Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1nk5","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-27t2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:00:40Z","created_by":"import"}]}
